subject: Monday Apr 13 — Week 1, Day 1: Lower A + Upper Push
html:
Week 1 of 15. Race day is 104 days out. Today is a gym day — Lower A + Upper Push, ~55 min. No running today; the knee gets another day to settle after Saturday's Cambria run.

Today's session: Lower A first (single-leg squat to bench, single-leg RDL, step-downs, Copenhagen plank, calf raises, wall sit), then Upper Push (bench, OHP, push-ups, lateral raise, tricep extension). Use the weights below — these are Week 1 starting points, not targets to exceed.

ExerciseWeightTarget feel
Single-leg squat to benchBodyweightForm focus — 3-sec descent
Single-leg RDL15 lbs/handGood (2–3 RIR)
Step-downsBodyweightForm focus — 3-sec descent
Copenhagen plankBodyweight20-sec holds each side
Calf raisesBodyweightFull ROM, slow lowering
Wall sitBodyweight30–45 sec
 
Dumbbell bench press30 lbs/handGood (2–3 RIR)
Overhead press20 lbs/handGood
Push-upsBodyweightGood
Lateral raise10 lbsGood
Tricep overhead extension20 lbsGood

Focus cue: The single-leg squat to bench is the most important exercise in the whole plan for your knee. Control a 3-second descent every single rep — don't drop into the bench. If form breaks down, cut reps before adding weight.

Knee watch: Saturday's run left your knee tingly and tender (Yellow). If you feel any sharpness during step-downs or wall sits today, stop that exercise and note it. No heroics on Day 1.

Context: Week 1 is establishment — the knee, the weights, the routine. Easy 5 mi tomorrow (HR under 140, ~9:30–10:00/mi). Foster City 5K in 19 days will recalibrate your MP targets.
